meaning: adj.欢呼的,喜悦的,喜洋洋的
examples: Suddenly, high above the jubilant roar and thunder of the revel, broke the clear peal of a bugle-note.
The team were greeted by thousands of jubilant supporters.
Hundreds of thousands of jubilant Americans cheered him on his return.
England paceman Kirtley, who was sitting out of the game with shin splints, was suitably jubilant.
We received the jubilant news from Roland Berger Strategy Consultants barely 48 hours ago.
